content:
# Revelation Chapter 19 -- The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Codex Sinaiticus, pages 291-292 | **Source Image:** REV_019_page_001.jpg, REV_019_page_002.jpg

1. After these things I heard something like the loud voice of a great multitude in heaven, saying, "Hallelujah! Salvation and glory and power belong to our God,

2. for true and righteous are his judgments; for he has judged the great harlot who was corrupting the earth with her fornication, and he has avenged the blood of his servants from her hand."

3. And a second time they said, "Hallelujah! And her smoke rises up forever and ever."

4. And the twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshiped God who sits upon the throne, saying, "Amen. Hallelujah!"

5. And a voice came from the throne, saying, "Praise our God, all you his servants, and those who fear him, the small and the great."

6. And I heard something like the voice of a great multitude and like the sound of many waters and like the sound of mighty thunders, saying, "Hallelujah! For the Lord our God, the Almighty, reigns.

7. Let us rejoice and be glad and give him the glory, for the marriage of the Lamb has come, and his bride has made herself ready."

8. And it was given to her to clothe herself in fine linen, bright and clean; for the fine linen is the righteous deeds of the saints.

9. And he says to me, "Write: 'Blessed are those who are called to the marriage supper of the Lamb.'" And he says to me, "These are the true words of God."

10. And I fell before his feet to worship him. And he says to me, "Do not do that! I am a fellow servant of yours and of your brothers who hold the testimony of Jesus. Worship God! For the testimony of Jesus is the spirit of prophecy."

11. And I saw heaven opened, and behold, a white horse, and the one sitting upon it is called Faithful and True, and in righteousness he judges and makes war.

12. And his eyes are like a flame of fire, and upon his head are many diadems, having a name written that no one knows except himself,

13. and he is clothed in a garment dipped in blood, and his name is called The Word of God.

14. And the armies that are in heaven were following him on white horses, clothed in fine linen, white and clean.

15. And from his mouth comes a sharp sword, so that with it he may strike the nations; and he will shepherd them with a rod of iron; and he treads the winepress of the wrath of the anger of God the Almighty.

16. And he has on his garment and on his thigh a name written: KING OF KINGS AND LORD OF LORDS.

17. And I saw one angel standing in the sun, and he cried out with a loud voice, saying to all the birds that fly in mid-heaven, "Come, gather together for the great supper of God,

18. so that you may eat the flesh of kings and the flesh of commanders and the flesh of mighty men, and the flesh of horses and of those who sit upon them, and the flesh of all men, both free and slave, and small and great."

19. And I saw the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies gathered together to make war against the one who sits upon the horse and against his army.

20. And the beast was seized, and with it the false prophet who had performed the signs before it, by which he deceived those who had received the mark of the beast and those who worshiped its image. These two were thrown alive into the lake of fire that burns with sulfur.

21. And the rest were killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the one who sits upon the horse, and all the birds were filled with their flesh.

---
## SOPHIA's Council Notes

The following verses achieved substantial agreement across all three translators:

- **19:4** -- All three agreed on the twenty-four elders and four living creatures falling down in worship, saying "Amen. Hallelujah!" Minor word order differences only.
- **19:8** -- All three agreed that the fine linen represents the righteous deeds of the saints/holy ones, with only the term for the saints differing.
- **19:11** -- Strong agreement on the heaven-opened vision of the white horse rider called Faithful and True who judges and makes war in righteousness.
- **19:13** -- All three agreed on the garment "dipped in blood" (following Sinaiticus *bebammenon*) and the name "The Word of God."
- **19:14** -- Universal agreement on heavenly armies following on white horses in white, clean linen.
- **19:16** -- All three agreed on the title KING OF KINGS AND LORD OF LORDS written on garment and thigh.
- **19:19** -- All three agreed on the beast, kings, and armies gathering for war against the rider.
- **19:20** -- Strong structural agreement on the beast and false prophet being thrown alive into the lake of fire burning with sulfur.

---
## Disagreement Log

**Verse 1:**
- EMET: "I heard as a great voice of a great multitude in the heaven, saying, Hallelujah! The salvation and the glory and the power of the God of us"
- LOGOS: "I heard something like a loud voice of a great multitude in heaven, saying, 'Hallelujah! The salvation and the glory and the power belong to our God'"
- PNEUMA: "I heard what sounded like the roar of a vast crowd in heaven, shouting: 'Hallelujah! Salvation, glory, and power belong to our God'"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS with minor adjustments. "Something like" for the Greek *hos*. "Salvation and glory and power" without definite articles (smoothing EMET's "the salvation and the glory and the power" while preserving the triad). PNEUMA's "roar of a vast crowd" and "shouting" are too interpretive for *phone megale* and *legonton*.

**Verse 2:**
- EMET: "he judged the great harlot, who was corrupting the earth with the fornication of her, and he avenged the blood of the slaves of him from the hand of her"
- LOGOS: "he has judged the great harlot who was corrupting the earth with her fornication, and he has avenged the blood of his servants from her hand"
- PNEUMA: "He has condemned the great prostitute who was poisoning the earth with her sexual immorality. He has avenged the blood of his servants that was on her hands"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Judged" over "condemned" (PNEUMA) for *ekrinen*. "Harlot" over "prostitute." "Fornication" over "sexual immorality." "Servants" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) over "slaves" (EMET) for *doulon* in this context -- both are valid, but "servants" is the established English convention for God's *douloi*. PNEUMA's "poisoning" for *ephtheiren* is interpretive.

**Verse 3:**
- EMET: "a second [time] they have said" (perfect tense)
- LOGOS: "a second time they said"
- PNEUMA: "a second time they shouted"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. Simple past "said" for narrative flow; PNEUMA's "shouted" adds intensity not in *eirekan*. EMET's note about the perfect tense is acknowledged but the English past tense suffices.

**Verse 5:**
- EMET: "Praise the God of us, all the slaves of him, and the [ones] fearing him, the small and the great"
- LOGOS: "Praise our God, all his servants, and those who fear him, the small and the great"
- PNEUMA: "Praise our God, all you who serve him -- all who stand in awe of him, from the least to the greatest"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "Praise our God, all you his servants, and those who fear him, the small and the great." "Our God" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) over EMET's literalistic "the God of us." "Fear" (EMET/LOGOS) over "stand in awe" (PNEUMA) for *phoboumenoi*. "The small and the great" (EMET/LOGOS) over "from the least to the greatest" (PNEUMA).

**Verse 6:**
- EMET: "as a voice of a great multitude, and as a voice of many waters, and as a voice of strong thunders, saying, Hallelujah! Because [the] Lord the God of us, the Almighty, reigned."
- LOGOS: "something like the voice of a great multitude and like the sound of many waters and like the sound of mighty thunders, saying, 'Hallelujah! For the Lord our God, the Almighty, reigns.'"
- PNEUMA: "what sounded like the roar of an enormous crowd, like the thunder of mighty waters, like the crash of powerful thunder, shouting: 'Hallelujah! For the Lord our God, the Almighty, has begun his reign!'"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Sound" for *phone* in the water/thunder similes (LOGOS) is appropriate. "Reigns" (LOGOS) for the ingressive aorist *ebasileusen* -- while PNEUMA's "has begun his reign" captures the ingressive aspect and EMET's "reigned" is the surface form, the present "reigns" conveys the inaugurated and ongoing reign. PNEUMA's varied terms ("roar," "thunder," "crash") depart from the repeated *phone* in the Greek.

**Verse 7:**
- EMET: "the wife of him prepared herself"
- LOGOS: "his wife has made herself ready"
- PNEUMA: "his bride has made herself ready"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "his bride has made herself ready." While *gyne* literally means "wife/woman" (EMET/LOGOS), in the context of a marriage that "has come," "bride" (PNEUMA) is the contextually appropriate English rendering. "Made herself ready" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) over "prepared herself" (EMET).

**Verse 9:**
- EMET: "Blessed [are] the [ones] to the supper of the marriage of the Lamb having been called. And he says to me, These the true words of the God are."
- LOGOS: "Blessed are those who are called to the marriage supper of the Lamb. And he says to me, 'These are the true words of God.'"
- PNEUMA: "Blessed are those who are invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb. And he added, 'These are the true words of God.'"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Called" (EMET/LOGOS) over "invited" (PNEUMA) for *keklemenoi* -- "called" preserves the theological weight. "Marriage supper" (LOGOS) over "supper of the marriage" (EMET) or "wedding feast" (PNEUMA).

**Verse 10:**
- EMET: "See [that you do] not! A fellow-slave of you I am and of the brothers of you, the [ones] having the testimony of Yeshua. Worship the God!"
- LOGOS: "See that you do not do that; I am a fellow servant of yours and of your brothers who hold the testimony of Jesus. Worship God."
- PNEUMA: "Do not do that! I am a fellow servant with you and with your brothers and sisters who hold to the testimony of Jesus. Worship God!"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA blends: "Do not do that!" (PNEUMA, clearer) + "I am a fellow servant of yours and of your brothers who hold the testimony of Jesus. Worship God!" (LOGOS). "Brothers" (EMET/LOGOS) without "and sisters" (PNEUMA) -- the Greek *adelphon* is masculine plural. "Jesus" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) over "Yeshua" (EMET). PNEUMA's "the spirit that animates all prophecy" is paraphrastic; SOPHIA retains "the spirit of prophecy" (EMET/LOGOS).

**Verse 12:**
- EMET: "the eyes of him [are] a flame of fire"
- LOGOS: "his eyes are like a flame of fire"
- PNEUMA: "His eyes blaze like fire"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS with "like a flame of fire." The Greek *hos phlox pyros* includes the comparative *hos*. "Diadems" (EMET/LOGOS) over "royal crowns" (PNEUMA) for *diademata*.

**Verse 15:**
- EMET: "he treads the winepress of the wine of the wrath of the anger of the God, the Almighty"
- LOGOS: "he treads the winepress of the wrath of the anger of God the Almighty"
- PNEUMA: "He treads the winepress of the furious wrath of God the Almighty"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. The double expression "wrath of the anger" (*tou thymou tes orges*) is preserved as two distinct terms (EMET/LOGOS). PNEUMA's "furious wrath" collapses the two Greek words into one phrase. LOGOS correctly omits "wine of" which EMET includes -- the Sinaiticus reads *tou thymou tes orges* without *tou oinou*.

**Verse 17:**
- EMET: "one angel standing in the sun... Come, gather together unto the great supper of the God"
- LOGOS: "one angel standing in the sun... Come, gather together for the great supper of God"
- PNEUMA: "an angel standing in the sun... Come! Gather for God's great feast!"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "One angel" (EMET/LOGOS) preserves the Sinaiticus numeral *hena aggelon*. "Supper" (EMET/LOGOS) for *deipnon* over "feast" (PNEUMA).

**Verse 18:**
- EMET: "flesh of commanders... flesh of strong [ones]... flesh of all, both free and slaves, and small and great"
- LOGOS: "the flesh of commanders and the flesh of mighty men... the flesh of all men, both free and slave, and small and great"
- PNEUMA: "the flesh of generals, the flesh of the powerful... the flesh of all people -- free and slave, small and great alike"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Commanders" (EMET/LOGOS) for *chiliarchon*. "Mighty men" (LOGOS) for *ischyron* over "strong ones" (EMET) or "the powerful" (PNEUMA).

**Verse 20:**
- EMET: "Living, the two were cast into the lake of the fire, the [one] burning with sulphur"
- LOGOS: "These two were thrown alive into the lake of fire that burns with sulfur"
- PNEUMA: "The two of them were thrown alive into the lake of fire that burns with sulfur"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Thrown" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) over "cast" (EMET). "These two" (LOGOS) is clear and accurate.

**Verse 21:**
- EMET: "the rest were killed with the sword of the [one] sitting upon the horse, the [sword] having come out from the mouth of him, and all the birds were filled from the flesh of them"
- LOGOS: "the rest were killed with the sword that came from the mouth of the one who sits upon the horse, and all the birds were filled with their flesh"
- PNEUMA: "The rest were killed by the sword that extended from the mouth of the rider on the horse, and all the birds gorged themselves on their flesh"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Killed with the sword" for the instrumental dative. "Were filled with their flesh" (LOGOS) over "gorged themselves" (PNEUMA, too vivid for *echortasthesan*) or EMET's "were filled from the flesh of them" (awkward preposition).

-- SOPHIA
